# OHZI Core — Instructions for Large Language Models

> OHZI Core (`ohzi-core` on npm) is a TypeScript graphics library built on top of Three.js.
> It provides a structured application lifecycle, scene management, asset loading,
> shader materials, post-processing, and rendering pipelines for WebGL/WebGPU projects.
> It is not standalone — it is designed to be used within the OHZI Boilerplate project.

## Project Identity

- **Name:** ohzi-core
- **Author:** OHZI Interactive Studio
- **License:** MIT
- **Language:** TypeScript (ES Modules)
- **Runtime dependency:** Three.js (0.183.0)
- **Renderer target:** Three.js WebGPU renderer
- **Repository:** https://github.com/ohzinteractive/core
- **Companion project:** https://github.com/ohzinteractive/boilerplate

## What This Library Does

OHZI Core gives you:

1. **A frame loop with lifecycle hooks** — Extend `BaseApplication` and override methods like `update()`, `on_pre_render()`, `fixed_update()`, etc. The `RenderLoop` class drives everything.
2. **A managed rendering pipeline** — `Graphics` wraps the Three.js renderer. Swap between render modes (Normal, Unreal Bloom, Normal+AO, Deferred, VR, Debug Normals) at runtime.
3. **Scene management with progressive loading** — `AbstractScene` provides a two-phase asset loading pipeline (regular + high-quality) with compilators that spread GPU upload work across frames.
4. **30+ custom shader materials** — SSAO, bloom, Gaussian blur, dual-filter blur, FXAA, deferred rendering, SDF text, depth/normal passes, and more.
5. **Asset loading** — 17 individual loaders (GLTF, DRACO, textures, HDR, cubemaps, audio, fonts, video, OBJ, DAE, etc.) plus async batch loaders. `ResourceContainer` deduplicates by URL.
6. **Post-processing utilities** — Gaussian blur, dual-filtering blur, median filter, depth/normals renderer, blitter, material passes.
7. **GPU particle system** — Simulation and rendering via data textures and custom shaders.
8. **Camera controller** — Pluggable movement modes and states for interactive camera control.
9. **View/UI layer** — `ViewManager`, `ViewComponent`, and `TransitionManager` for application state and animated transitions.
10. **Utilities** — Math helpers (`OMath`), easing functions, geometry utilities, camera fitting, mesh sampling, raycasting, SDF text rendering, canvas drawing, CSS animation, and more.

## Architecture Overview

### Core Singletons

These are instantiated once at module level and exported by name. They are initialized by `Initializer.init()`:

| Singleton | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `Graphics` | Wraps Three.js renderer; manages render modes, blitting, screenshots |
| `Time` | `delta_time`, `smooth_delta_time`, `elapsed_time`, fixed timestep (1/30) |
| `OScreen` | Viewport dimensions (logical and physical), DPR, aspect ratio, resize detection |
| `SceneManager` | Holds and disposes the active `AbstractScene` |
| `CameraManager` | Global access to the active camera (and VR spectator camera) |
| `Debug` | Debug visualization layer (3D shapes, textures, 2D overlays) |
| `ResourceContainer` | Asset cache keyed by name and URL |
| `OS` | Platform detection (mobile, iOS, Android, Mac, Windows, Linux) |
| `Browser` | Browser detection |
| `Capabilities` | GPU/feature capability detection |

### Frame Lifecycle

`RenderLoop` calls these hooks each frame in order:

```
Time.__update()
on_post_start()        ← second frame only
input.update()
before_update()
fixed_update()         ← runs in a loop at fixed 1/30s timestep
update()
ViewManager.update()
on_pre_render()
Graphics.update()      ← renders the scene via the active render mode
on_post_render()
Debug.render()
on_frame_end()
```

Users implement these by extending `BaseApplication`:

```typescript
import { BaseApplication, RenderLoop, Graphics, Initializer } from 'ohzi-core';

class MyApp extends BaseApplication {
  on_enter(loop: RenderLoop) { /* setup */ }
  update()                   { /* per-frame logic */ }
  on_pre_render()            { /* before render */ }
  on_post_render()           { /* after render */ }
  fixed_update()             { /* physics at 1/30 */ }
  on_exit(loop: RenderLoop)  { /* cleanup */ }
}
```

### Initialization Sequence

```typescript
import { Initializer, Graphics, RenderLoop } from 'ohzi-core';

// 1. Initialize core singletons
Initializer.init(input);

// 2. Initialize graphics (requires a renderer instance)
Graphics.init({ renderer, core_attributes: { xr_enabled: false }, dpr });

// 3. Create and start the render loop
const app = new MyApp();
const loop = new RenderLoop(app, Graphics, input);
loop.start();
```

### Rendering Pipeline

`Graphics` supports multiple render modes via `Graphics.set_state()`:

| Render Mode | Description |
|---|---|
| `NormalRender` | Standard forward rendering |
| `UnrealBloomRender` | Forward rendering with Unreal-style bloom post-processing |
| `NormalAORender` | Forward rendering with SSAO |
| `BloomRender` | Basic bloom rendering |
| `DeferredRender` | Deferred rendering pipeline |
| `VRRender` | WebXR stereo rendering |
| `DebugNormalsRender` | Visualizes surface normals |

All render modes extend `BaseRender` and implement a `render()` method called by `Graphics.update()`.

### Scene & Asset Loading

`AbstractScene` extends Three.js `Scene` with a two-phase loading pipeline:

```
load() → init() → RegularLoadingState → on_assets_ready() → compilators → on_assets_compiled()
                 → HighQualityLoadingState → on_high_quality_assets_ready()
```

Individual asset loaders (all in `src/resource_loader/`):

| Loader | Formats |
|---|---|
| `GLTFLoader` | .gltf, .glb |
| `GLTFDRACOLoader` | DRACO-compressed GLTF |
| `TextureLoader` | .png, .jpg, .webp, etc. |
| `HDRTextureLoader` | .hdr |
| `HDRCubeTextureLoader` | HDR cubemaps |
| `CubemapLoader` | Standard cubemaps |
| `BasisLoader` | Basis Universal textures |
| `AudioLoader` | Audio files |
| `VideoLoader` | Video files |
| `FontLoader` | Font files |
| `OBJLoader` | .obj |
| `DAELoader` | .dae (Collada) |
| `JSONLoader` | JSON data |
| `FileLoader` | Raw files |
| `TextLoader` | Text files |
| `PointArrayLoader` | Point cloud data |

Batch loaders (`AsyncTexturesLoader`, `AsyncObjectsLoader`, `AsyncAudiosLoader`) handle groups of assets. `ResourceContainer` caches by URL to prevent duplicate loads.

### Shader Materials

25+ custom `ShaderMaterial` subclasses, all extending `BaseShaderMaterial`. GLSL files are organized in `src/shaders/` by feature and inlined at build time via `rollup-plugin-glslify`.

Key materials: `SSAOMaterial`, `SSAOComposeMaterial`, `GaussianBlurMaterial`, `DualFilteringBlurMaterial`, `FXAAMaterial`, `LuminosityHighPassMaterial`, `UnrealBloomComposeMaterial`, `DepthNormalMaterial`, `SDFTextMaterial`, `SDFScreenTextMaterial`, `DeferredRendererComposeMaterial`, `BlitMaterial`, `AddMaterial`, `MedianFilterMaterial`.

### Post-Processing Utilities

| Utility | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `GaussianBlurrer` | Multi-pass Gaussian blur on render targets |
| `DualFilteringBlurrer` | Kawase dual-filter blur (faster, lower quality) |
| `MedianFilter` | Median filter pass |
| `Blurrer` | Generic blur interface |
| `DepthAndNormalsRenderer` | Generates depth/normal textures for SSAO and effects |
| `Blitter` | Copies render targets with optional material passes |

### Components

Renderable components in `src/components/`:

| Component | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `Grid` | Debug grid |
| `Line` | Line rendering |
| `Text2D` | Screen-space text |
| `SDFTextBatch` | SDF-based text rendering (resolution-independent) |
| `WorldImage` | Image plane in world space |
| `UpdatableMaterialMesh` | Mesh with dynamically-updated material |
| `Shape2D`, `Shape3D` | Shape primitives |
| `MultiLinePath` | Multi-segment line paths |

### View & Transition System

| Class | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `ViewManager` | Manages application views/screens |
| `ViewComponent` | Base class for UI view components |
| `ViewComponentManager` | Manages view component lifecycle |
| `ApplicationView` | Base class for application views |
| `TransitionManager` | Manages animated transitions between states |
| `TransitionTable` | Defines valid transitions |
| `ActionSequencer` | Event sequencing with time-based interpolators |

### Primitives

Ready-to-use 3D primitives: `Arrow`, `Cube`, `Sphere`, `HorizontalPlane`, `VerticalPlane`.

### Utilities

| Utility | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `OMath` | Extended math functions (lerp, clamp, map, etc.) |
| `EasingFunctions` | Standard easing curves for animation |
| `CameraUtilities` | Camera positioning, fitting, projection helpers |
| `FrustumPointFitter` | Fits points within camera frustum |
| `GeometryUtilities` | Geometry manipulation helpers |
| `MeshSampler` | Random point sampling on mesh surfaces |
| `ModelUtilities` | GLTF model processing helpers |
| `ArrayUtilities` | Array manipulation helpers |
| `StringUtilities` | String helpers |
| `TimeUtilities` | Time formatting helpers |
| `ImageUtilities` | Image processing helpers |
| `HTMLUtilities` | DOM manipulation helpers |
| `ObjectUtilities` | Object/map helpers |
| `Validation` | Input validation |

### GPU Particles

`GPUParticleSystem` provides GPU-accelerated particle simulation and rendering using data textures and custom shaders. Configured via `ParticleAttribute` and `ParticlePositionAttribute`.

### Camera Controller

`CameraController` provides interactive camera control with pluggable movement modes (`CameraMovementMode`, `ImmediateMode`) and state machines (`AbstractCameraState`, `CommonCameraState`).

## Source Directory Structure

```
src/
  index.ts                    Main entry point (re-exports everything)
  BaseApplication.ts          Base class users extend
  RenderLoop.ts               Frame loop driver
  Graphics.ts                 Renderer wrapper (singleton)
  Time.ts                     Timing system (singleton)
  OScreen.ts                  Screen metrics (singleton)
  SceneManager.ts             Scene management (singleton)
  CameraManager.ts            Camera access (singleton)
  Initializer.ts              Bootstrap/teardown (singleton)
  Debug.ts                    Debug visualization (singleton)
  ResourceContainer.ts        Asset cache (singleton)
  OS.ts                       Platform detection (singleton)
  Browser.ts                  Browser detection
  Capabilities.ts             GPU capabilities
  KeyboardInput.ts            Keyboard state tracking
  PerspectiveCamera.ts        Extended Three.js PerspectiveCamera
  OrthographicCamera.ts       Extended Three.js OrthographicCamera
  Screen.ts                   Screen utilities
  action_sequencer/           Event sequencing and interpolators
  camera_controller/          Camera controller with movement modes
  canvas_drawer/              Canvas 2D drawing utilities
  components/                 Renderable components (Grid, Text2D, SDF, etc.)
  data_textures/              Data texture utilities
  gpu_particles/              GPU particle system
  html_utilities/             CSS animation helpers
  lib/                        Internal library code
  loaders/                    Async batch loaders
  materials/                  25+ custom ShaderMaterials
  primitives/                 Geometric primitives
  raycast/                    Ray intersection utilities
  render_mode/                Rendering strategies
  render_utilities/           Post-processing (blur, depth, blit)
  resource_loader/            17 individual asset loaders
  scenes/                     AbstractScene and loading states
  shaders/                    GLSL shader files (26 feature folders)
  utilities/                  Math, easing, geometry, camera helpers
  view_components/            View/UI layer with transitions
```

## Build System

- **Bundler:** Rollup
- **Output:** `build/index.mjs` (ES module with sourcemaps, minified with terser)
- **Types:** `types/index.d.ts` (generated separately via `yarn generate-types`)
- **GLSL:** Inlined via `rollup-plugin-glslify`
- **Externals:** `three` and its sub-modules are not bundled

## Commands

```bash
yarn start            # Development watch mode
yarn build            # Production build
yarn generate-types   # Generate TypeScript declarations
yarn fix-syntax       # Lint and auto-fix with ESLint
yarn upload           # Build, publish to npm, and create git tag
```

## Key Design Patterns

1. **Singletons everywhere** — Core services are instantiated at module level and exported. Access them directly: `Time.delta_time`, `Graphics.render()`, `OScreen.width`, etc.
2. **Lifecycle hooks** — Extend `BaseApplication` and override only the hooks you need. The framework calls them in the correct order.
3. **Render mode swapping** — Change the entire rendering pipeline at runtime via `Graphics.set_state(new UnrealBloomRender())`.
4. **Two-phase asset loading** — Regular assets load first (with compilators spreading GPU work across frames), then high-quality assets load in the background.
5. **Fixed timestep** — Physics and deterministic logic runs in `fixed_update()` at 1/30s, with `frame_interpolation` available for smooth visual interpolation.
6. **State machine application** — `RenderLoop.set_state()` swaps the active `BaseApplication` instance FSM-style, calling `on_exit`/`on_enter`.
